reasoning: Applied error taxonomy to 25 classified errors. Selected 7 questions targeting the highest-priority error patterns: 3rd person -s (exercise_priority 5, 8 occurrences), null subject 'it' (priority 4-5, 5 occurrences), false friends (priority 4, 2 occurrences), prepositions (priority 3, 4 occurrences), there is/are agreement (priority 3), and age construction (priority 5). ZPD calibration based on A1+ level assessment: zpd_lower targets recognition of rules already corrected in lesson (boolean, choice), zpd_target tests application in context (gap with choices, sorter), zpd_stretch requires production recall (gap with blank). Difficulty distribution: 2 zpd_lower (29%), 3 zpd_target (43%), 2 zpd_stretch (29%). Distractors use Carmen's actual errors, L1 transfer patterns from Spanish, and common developmental forms at A1.

source_ref.original: My father work in a hospital
source_ref.corrected: My father works in a hospital

payload.title: Third person -s: complete the sentence
payload.template_text: My father {0} in a hospital. He {1} people every day.
payload.explanation: With he/she/it, add -s to the verb in present simple: he works, he helps.
payload.content: Your original sentence: 'My father work in a hospital'

payload.title: Which sentence is correct?
payload.explanation: With he/she/it, the negative is 'doesn't' (does + not), not 'don't'.
payload.content: Your original sentence: 'My mother don't like cats'

source_ref.original: Is very cold today in London
source_ref.corrected: It is very cold today in London

payload.title: Add the missing subject
payload.template_text: {0} is very cold today. {1} is because we are in London in winter.
payload.explanation: English always needs a subject. Use 'it' as a dummy subject for weather, time, and impersonal statements: 'It is cold', 'It is because...'
payload.content: Your original sentences: 'Is very cold today' and 'Is because I practice a lot'

source_ref.original: Actually I live in London for three months
source_ref.corrected: Currently I live in London for three months

payload.title: 'Actually' in English means 'actualmente' (currently)
payload.explanation: 'Actually' means 'en realidad' (in fact). To say 'actualmente', use 'currently'. This is a common false friend between Spanish and English.

payload.title: How do we express age in English?
payload.template_text: My sister {0} sixteen years old. My grandmother {1} eighty years old.
payload.explanation: In English, we use 'be' for age: 'she IS sixteen years old.' In Spanish you say 'tiene dieciseis anos' (has sixteen years), but English uses 'is' instead of 'has'.
payload.content: Your original sentence: 'She have sixteen years'

source_ref.original: My mother is a woman very nice
source_ref.corrected: My mother is a very nice woman

payload.title: Arrange the words to describe Carmen's mother
payload.explanation: In English, adjectives go BEFORE the noun: 'a very nice woman', not 'a woman very nice'. In Spanish it is the opposite: 'una mujer muy simpática'.

payload.title: Complete with the correct form of 'have'
payload.template_text: My aunt {0} two cats. She {1} a big house with a garden.
payload.explanation: With he/she/it, 'have' becomes 'has'. 'My aunt has two cats. She has a big house.'
payload.content: Your original sentence: 'she have two cats'
